steps.title: Read what the colon is introducing
steps.content: The blank describes how the tree-planting approach turned out, and the colon introduces the evidence: the bullfinch population rose from about 100 birds to roughly 1,300. That is a thirteenfold increase — clear proof the approach achieved its stated goal of boosting numbers.

steps.title: Pick the word that matches the evidence
steps.content: An approach that meets its goal so dramatically is, by definition, successful. "Successful" is the precise label for an effort that produced the desired result.

steps.title: Rule out the alternatives
steps.content:
• Choice A — amusing: conservation results aren't a matter of humor; the evidence is about effectiveness, not entertainment. ✗
  • Choice B — costly: the passage gives no dollar figures or resource expenditures — cost isn't what the colon's evidence supports. ✗
  • Choice C — successful: directly matches a 100-to-1,300 population jump. ✓
  • Choice D — disastrous: flatly contradicts a thirteenfold population increase. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
